summary: U.S.-Iran peace talks flickered this week — Pakistan reportedly delivered a 15-point U.S. proposal to Tehran, and Iran signaled it would allow "non-hostile" ships through the Strait of Hormuz — but Iran denied any negotiations and rejected a ceasefire, keeping energy risk premiums elevated and bonds under pressure.
tweet: Iran rejected a U.S. peace plan while new Section 301 trade probes across a dozen economies keep policy risk front and center — fear is the base case.
